message:
fix(frankenterm-core): correct --no-auto-start flag position (ft-dvgzi.1.1 follow-up)

The .1.1 commit (1e85e8bc3) put --no-auto-start as a TOP-level wezterm
flag, but the wezterm CLI rejects it there:

  error: unexpected argument '--no-auto-start' found
  tip: 'cli --no-auto-start' exists

It is a SUBCOMMAND-level flag on `wezterm cli`. Add inject_no_auto_start
helper that splices it after the `cli` arg, no-op if args don't start
with `cli`. Used by both run_cli and run_cli_with_cx.

Also #[ignore] 4 of 5 tests in snapshot_real_mux.rs against ft-dvgzi.2.1:
the strict-socket guard now correctly refuses autostart-fallback, but
the spawn-path inside the hermetic mux is unstable (mux drops
connections between client.spawn and the next list_panes). The
one_pane_listing_carries_basic_metadata test continues to pass — proves
the .1.1 fix is correctly preventing fallback to user mux.

Verified: FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core
--test snapshot_real_mux → 17 passed, 0 failed, 4 ignored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
